english_name: The First Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Corinthians

verses.latin_vulgate: Si linguis hominum loquar, et angelorum, caritatem autem non habeam, factus sum velut æs sonans, aut cymbalum tinniens.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν ταῖς γλώσσαις τῶν ἀνθρώπων λαλῶ καὶ τῶν ἀγγέλων ἀγάπην δὲ μὴ ἔχω γέγονα χαλκὸς ἠχῶν ἢ κύμβαλον ἀλαλάζον
verses.king_james: Though I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, and have not charity, I am become *as* sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si habuero prophetiam, et noverim mysteria omnia, et omnem scientiam: et si habuero omnem fidem ita ut montes transferam, caritatem autem non habuero, nihil sum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐὰν ἔχω προφητείαν καὶ εἰδῶ τὰ μυστήρια πάντα καὶ πᾶσαν τὴν γνῶσιν καὶ ἐὰν ἔχω πᾶσαν τὴν πίστιν ὥστε ὄρη μεθιστάνειν ἀγάπην δὲ μὴ ἔχω οὐθέν εἰμι
verses.king_james: And though I have *the gift of* prophecy, and understand all mysteries, and all knowledge; and though I have all faith, so that I could remove mountains, and have not charity, I am nothing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si distribuero in cibos pauperum omnes facultates meas, et si tradidero corpus meum ita ut ardeam, caritatem autem non habuero, nihil mihi prodest.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐὰν ψωμίσω πάντα τὰ ὑπάρχοντά μου καὶ ἐὰν παραδῶ τὸ σῶμά μου ἵνα καυθήσωμαι ἀγάπην δὲ μὴ ἔχω οὐδὲν ὠφελοῦμαι
verses.king_james: And though I bestow all my goods to feed *the poor*, and though I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Caritas patiens est, benigna est. Caritas non æmulatur, non agit perperam, non inflatur,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ ἀγάπη μακροθυμεῖ χρηστεύεται ἡ ἀγάπη οὐ ζηλοῖ ἡ ἀγάπη οὐ περπερεύεται οὐ φυσιοῦται
verses.king_james: Charity suffereth long, *and* is kind; charity envieth not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up,

verses.latin_vulgate: non est ambitiosa, non quærit quæ sua sunt, non irritatur, non cogitat malum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἀσχημονεῖ οὐ ζητεῖ τὰ ἑαυτῆς οὐ παροξύνεται οὐ λογίζεται τὸ κακόν
verses.king_james: Doth not behave itself unseemly, seeketh not her own, is not easily provoked, thinketh no evil;

verses.latin_vulgate: non gaudet super iniquitate, congaudet autem veritati:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ χαίρει ἐπὶ τῇ ἀδικίᾳ συγχαίρει δὲ τῇ ἀληθείᾳ
verses.king_james: Rejoiceth not in iniquity, but rejoiceth in the truth;

verses.latin_vulgate: omnia suffert, omnia credit, omnia sperat, omnia sustinet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντα στέγει πάντα πιστεύει πάντα ἐλπίζει πάντα ὑπομένει
verses.king_james: Beareth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, endureth all things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Caritas numquam excidit: sive prophetiæ evacuabuntur, sive linguæ cessabunt, sive scientia destruetur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ ἀγάπη οὐδέποτε ἐκπίπτει εἴτε δὲ προφητεῖαι καταργηθήσονται εἴτε γλῶσσαι παύσονται εἴτε γνῶσις καταργηθήσεται
verses.king_james: Charity never faileth: but whether *there be* prophecies, they shall fail; whether *there be* tongues, they shall cease; whether *there be* knowledge, it shall vanish away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ex parte enim cognoscimus, et ex parte prophetamus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκ μέρους γὰρ γινώσκομεν καὶ ἐκ μέρους προφητεύομεν
verses.king_james: For we know in part, and we prophesy in part.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem venerit quod perfectum est, evacuabitur quod ex parte est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν δὲ ἔλθῃ τὸ τέλειον τότε τὸ ἐκ μέρους καταργηθήσεται
verses.king_james: But when that which is perfect is come, then that which is in part shall be done away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum essem parvulus, loquebar ut parvulus, cogitabam ut parvulus. Quando autem factus sum vir, evacuavi quæ erant parvuli.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε ἤμην νήπιος ὡς νήπιος ἐλάλουν ὡς νήπιος ἐφρόνουν ὡς νήπιος ἐλογιζόμην ὅτε δέ γέγονα ἀνήρ κατήργηκα τὰ τοῦ νηπίου
verses.king_james: When I was a child, I spake as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child: but when I became a man, I put away childish things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videmus nunc per speculum in ænigmate: tunc autem facie ad faciem. Nunc cognosco ex parte: tunc autem cognoscam sicut et cognitus sum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: βλέπομεν γὰρ ἄρτι δι᾽ ἐσόπτρου ἐν αἰνίγματι τότε δὲ πρόσωπον πρὸς πρόσωπον ἄρτι γινώσκω ἐκ μέρους τότε δὲ ἐπιγνώσομαι καθὼς καὶ ἐπεγνώσθην
verses.king_james: For now we see through a glass, darkly; but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc autem manent fides, spes, caritas, tria hæc: major autem horum est caritas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νυνὶ δὲ μένει πίστις ἐλπίς ἀγάπη τὰ τρία ταῦτα μείζων δὲ τούτων ἡ ἀγάπη
verses.king_james: And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these *is* charity.
